title: Dzukou Valley — The Valley of Flowers of the Northeast

season.notes: June–August is the Dzukou lily season — the valley floor turns completely white and pink. Counterintuitively this is also monsoon season, so expect rain. December–January offers snow and no crowds but biting cold (-5°C nights). April–May is dry and brown — nothing to see.

month_by_month.january: Snow possible. Very cold. Stunning but demanding. Few trekkers.
month_by_month.february: Cold but clearing. Early wildflowers starting.
month_by_month.april: Dry season. Valley looks parched. Not recommended.
month_by_month.june: Dzukou lilies begin blooming. Rain starts. Peak season begins.
month_by_month.july: Peak bloom. Valley completely covered. Rain daily but worth it.
month_by_month.august: Lilies still strong. Less rain than July.
month_by_month.september: Post-bloom. Green and lush. Underrated month.
month_by_month.october: Autumn colours. Quiet. Good trekking conditions.
month_by_month.november: Cool and clear. Last good window before winter.
month_by_month.december: Snow possible. Very cold. Adventurous option.

description: A high-altitude valley at 2,438m straddling Nagaland and Manipur. Famous for the Dzukou lily (Lilium mackliniae) which blankets the valley floor every monsoon. Accessible by two routes — from Nagaland side (Viswema village) or Manipur side (Mao).
incitetales_angle: Everyone says 'Valley of Flowers' and pictures Uttarakhand. Nobody talks about Dzukou. In July, this valley is more dramatic — and you'll share it with roughly zero tourists from outside the Northeast.

local_intel.how_to_get: Base camp is Viswema village, 20km from Kohima. Trek is 5km uphill, approximately 3–4 hours. Alternatively the Manipur side via Mao Gate is longer but less steep.
local_intel.entry_fee: ₹100 per person (Nagaland side). Additional ₹50 camera fee.
local_intel.permit_notes: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for non-Nagaland residents. Get it in Kohima DC office or online at nagaland.gov.in. Takes 1 day. Free of cost.
local_intel.best_time_of_day: Start trek by 6am to reach valley before afternoon cloud cover
local_intel.duration: 1 night minimum recommended. Day trek possible but not ideal.
local_intel.trek_duration: 4–5 hours to reach the valley camp from Viswema base

connectivity.notes: No mobile network inside the valley. Inform someone before you go. Last signal is at Viswema village base.

local_intel.food_cost_note: Food at the valley camp is slightly higher than normal — everything has to be carried up. Budget ₹200–350 per meal. Worth it, but don't be surprised.

operator.name: Dzukou Valley Trek Operators Association
operator.notes: Local guides available at Viswema village base. Recommended for first-timers. Basic guesthouse at valley top — book ahead in July.

what_most_people_miss: The ridge walk on day 2 toward Japfu Peak. Most people just do the valley floor and leave. The ridge offers a 360° view of both Nagaland and Manipur on a clear morning.
cultural_notes: Viswema is an Angami Naga village. The community manages the trek. Buy supplies from local shops — it directly supports them. Do not litter. The Nagas take this extremely seriously.
